## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation

**FED HOLD (Jan 2026):** Fed held rates at 3.50–3.75% in January after three 2025 cuts; two members dissented preferring another cut. **MEDIUM** | CONFIRMS momentum across portfolio. Market sees low odds of March cut; strategists still expect one cut in 2026.

**FEB CPI (Released March 11):** CPI-U rose 0.3% MoM (Jan: +0.2%), +2.4% YoY. Core CPI +0.2% MoM, +2.5% YoY — all in line with expectations. **MEDIUM** | CONFIRMS — inflation tracking to Fed target; no surprise.

**10-YEAR YIELD SPIKE:** 10Y Treasury yield climbed to 4.19% on March 11, driven by Feb CPI release against backdrop of geopolitical instability. "Bear steepening" — yields rising despite Fed holding steady; market pricing higher-for-longer inflation due to energy supply shocks rather than Fed hikes. **HIGH** | CONTRADICTS precious_metals momentum (gold/silver down despite falling real rates) — break of historical correlation. Real rates fell to late-Oct levels, yet gold is -13.3% from Jan peak.

**ENERGY SHOCK AHEAD:** Feb CPI predates recent oil price surge tied to Iran war. Gasoline inflation will show up in March data. "Calm before the storm." **HIGH** | NOVEL — energy-driven inflation spike not yet priced into equities or Fed expectations.

## 2. Geopolitical / Commodity

**VIX SPIKE:** VIX surged 3.25% to 25.74 on March 11, 2026 — breach of psychological 25 level signals high-volatility regime. Driven by escalating Middle East conflict and energy market swings. **HIGH** | CONTRADICTS risk-on positioning across ai_tech, strategic_materials. Market in defensive mode.

**STRAIT OF HORMUZ PARALYZED:** ~20% of world's daily oil supply blocked by naval mines and drone threats. Shipping insurance canceled; commercial tankers stranded. Geopolitical deadlock persists despite Trump admin "near end" comments. **HIGH** | NOVEL / CONTRADICTS — energy supply shock is structural, not transient. **INFLATIONARY RISK PREMIUM:** Investors demanding higher returns on bonds to compensate for eroding purchasing power as $100/barrel oil becomes distinct possibility. **HIGH** | NOVEL — bond yields rising for wrong reasons (inflation fear, not growth).

## 3. AI / Semiconductor

**US CHIP EXPORT CONTROLS (Reported March 5):** Drafted rules would require US gov approval to ship AI chips *anywhere* outside US (Nvidia, AMD). Gives US control over every export sale regardless of destination. **MEDIUM** | CONFIRMS long-term strategic importance of AI hardware; CONTRADICTS near-term trade fluidity. ai_tech sleeve (SOXQ, CHAT, BOTZ, DTCR, GRID) exposed to regulatory uncertainty.

**NVIDIA H200 POLICY SHIFT (Jan 15, 2026):** BIS revised license policy for H200/AMD MI325X from "presumption of denial" to "case-by-case review" for exports to China under strict conditions. Effective Jan 15. **MEDIUM** | CONFIRMS — easing from Biden-era restrictions, but with strings attached.

**NVIDIA SMUGGLING BUST (Dec 2025):** DOJ broke up smuggling ring attempting to export $160M of H200 chips to China — same model now allowed under new policy. **MEDIUM** | CONFIRMS enforcement gap risk; regulatory whiplash continues.

## 4. Energy / Materials

**URANIUM PRICES:** Spot uranium near $86.20/lb as of early March 2026, up +33% YoY (pullback from Jan peak $101.50). Macro models point to $92/lb within 12 months on structural supply tightness. **HIGH** | CONFIRMS strategic_materials momentum (URNM #1 rank). Supply concentrated in Kazakhstan (43% global output) with ongoing production constraints.

**NUCLEAR DEMAND SURGE:** WNA biennial forecast shows significantly more robust uranium growth to 2040. <cite index="77-1,77-2">Global reactor uranium demand expected to rise from ~68,920 tU in 2025 to >150,000 tU by 2040 (reference case), driven by AI data center power needs and energy security policy.</cite> **HIGH** | CONFIRMS URNM, strategic_materials sleeve. Long-dated structural demand; near-term supply deficit emerging by 2026.

**US URANIUM SECURITY (Jan 2026):** White House issued Section 232 proclamation formally designating uranium a national security risk, opening door to import restrictions, price floors, federal support for domestic miners. **HIGH** | CONFIRMS URNM, REMX, COPX positioning. Policy tailwind for North American producers.
